skill.name: stripe-best-practices
skill.description: Guides Stripe integration design and review across payments, Billing, Connect, Tax, Treasury, and security best practices.
skill.summary: Choose secure, current Stripe APIs and implementation patterns for common integration work.
skill.author: stripe

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, network access, weak cryptography, sensitive file references, and a dangerous combination heuristic. Manual review found Markdown guidance only: inline code examples, Stripe documentation links, and security terminology were misclassified. No executable scripts, credential exfiltration, prompt injection, or malicious behavior were found.

low_findings.title: Markdown Inline Code Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Backticked Stripe API names, parameter names, and TypeScript examples are documentation content, not Ruby or shell execution. Confidence: 0.96. Confidence reasoning: The reviewed files are Markdown references and contain no executable wrapper, script entry point, or instruction to run arbitrary commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files are Markdown guidance and the flagged backticks surround API names or examples. No execution path or command runner exists in the reviewed skill files.

low_findings.title: Stripe Documentation Links Misclassified as Runtime Network Access
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The hardcoded URLs point to Stripe documentation, Dashboard pages, or support pages used as reference links. They do not trigger network requests by the skill. Confidence: 0.94. Confidence reasoning: The URLs are static Markdown links and no code invokes fetch, curl, HTTP clients, or browser automation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are documentation citations in Markdown. No reviewed file contains runtime network code or secret-bearing outbound requests.

low_findings.title: Security Terminology Misclassified as Sensitive or Cryptographic Abuse
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Terms such as SAML, SCIM, `.md` documentation paths, and example Stripe key prefixes were flagged as weak cryptography, system reconnaissance, or Windows SAM database access. The content teaches safer key handling and identity controls. Confidence: 0.91. Confidence reasoning: The relevant lines are defensive guidance and documentation references, not file access, cryptographic implementation, or reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text appears in defensive security documentation and Stripe API guidance. No evidence found for Windows SAM access, weak crypto code, or system reconnaissance.

content.value_statement: Stripe integrations require current API choices, secure key handling, and careful product routing. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structured Stripe guidance for implementation and review.
actual_capabilities: Routes payment use cases to Checkout Sessions, PaymentIntents, Setup Intents, or Billing APIs.
Explains Connect platform choices using Accounts v2 and controller properties.
Recommends secure Stripe key handling with restricted keys, vault storage, rotation, and webhook verification.
Identifies deprecated Stripe APIs and suggests current migration paths.
Guides Stripe Tax, subscription, and Treasury integration decisions using focused reference files.
Highlights payment method configuration patterns that support dynamic payment methods.
limitations: Does not call Stripe APIs or validate a live account configuration.
Does not replace legal, tax, PCI, or financial compliance advice.
Does not guarantee that every Stripe documentation link is current after publication.
Requires users to provide their integration context, region, and product requirements.

use_cases.title: Design a Payment Flow
use_cases.description: Select the right Stripe API surface for one-time payments, saved payment methods, subscriptions, or custom checkout.

use_cases.title: Review Marketplace Architecture
use_cases.description: Check Connect account setup, liability choices, charge types, and onboarding patterns before implementation.

use_cases.title: Harden Stripe Security
use_cases.description: Review key storage, restricted keys, webhook signatures, OAuth state handling, and incident response steps.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Stripe API
prompt_templates.prompt: I am building a Stripe integration for [use case]. Recommend the correct Stripe API surface and explain why.

prompt_templates.title: Review Payment Code
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Stripe payment flow for outdated APIs, unsafe payment method settings, and missing security steps.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Connect Marketplace
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a Stripe Connect setup for a marketplace with [business model], [liability preference], and [onboarding needs].

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Launch Checklist
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this Stripe launch plan for API version choices, key management, webhooks, tax setup, and migration risks.

output_examples.input: Which Stripe API should I use for a SaaS subscription checkout?
output_examples.output: Use Billing APIs with Checkout Sessions in subscription mode. This handles initial payment, trials, proration, and recurring invoice behavior.

output_examples.input: Can I hardcode card-only payment methods for my PaymentIntent?
output_examples.output: Avoid card-only payment method settings for online payments. Use dynamic payment methods unless you are building a Terminal integration.

output_examples.input: How should I protect a Stripe secret key used by a backend job?
output_examples.output: Use a restricted API key with only required permissions. Store it in a secrets vault, rotate it when needed, and never log it.

best_practices: Read the relevant reference file before answering a Stripe implementation question.
Prefer restricted API keys and least-privilege permissions for backend services.
Recommend current Stripe APIs instead of legacy Charges, Sources, Card Element, or v1 Connect patterns.
anti_patterns: Do not put Stripe secret keys or restricted keys in client-side code.
Do not recommend raw PaymentIntents for subscription renewal loops.
Do not hardcode online payment method types when dynamic payment methods are available.

faq.question: Does this skill run Stripe API calls?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and review support, but it does not call Stripe APIs.

faq.question: Can it help choose between Checkout and PaymentIntents?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains when to use Checkout Sessions, PaymentIntents, Setup Intents, Billing APIs, or higher-level products.

faq.question: Does it cover Stripe Connect?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers Accounts v2, controller properties, charge types, onboarding, and platform responsibility decisions.

faq.question: Does it provide tax advice?
faq.answer: No. It explains Stripe Tax integration patterns, but legal and tax obligations require qualified advice.

faq.question: Can it review security practices?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers key storage, restricted keys, webhook verification, OAuth state, 2FA, SAML, and incident response.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
